title: Optimizing a Memoized React Function Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References

statement.markdown: A memoized React function component is designed to optimize performance by caching the results of expensive function calls and reusing them when the same inputs occur again. However, this approach can lead to unexpected behavior when the underlying data structure changes, causing the component to re-render unnecessarily. Your task is to optimize the memoization logic to prevent the component from re-rendering due to changing array references.
statement.inputFormat: The input is an object with an array property `items` and a method `updateItems` that modifies the array.
statement.outputFormat: The output is the optimized memoized function component that prevents re-renders due to changing array references.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the actual data changes, not when the array reference changes.

examples.explanation: The component should only re-render when the actual data changes.
The component should re-render when the actual data changes.

hints.text: Understand the memoization logic and its limitations
Use a deep equality check to compare the actual data
Optimize the component to re-render only when the actual data changes

editorial.title: Optimizing Memoized React Function Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: Memoization can lead to unexpected behavior when the underlying data structure changes.
editorial.bruteForce: Simple memoization logic can lead to re-renders due to changing array references.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a deep equality check to compare the actual data.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimize the component to re-render only when the actual data changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the actual data changes.
